From 1871 through 1946, the Tatsuta Shrine was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. ==Overview== == Tatsuta Taisha == {{otheruses|Shrine in Sangō, Ikoma District, Nara Prefecture|Shrine in Ikaruga, Ikoma District, Nara Prefecture|{{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|lt=Tatsuta Shrine|ja|龍田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|qq=}}}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tatsuta Taisha |image = [[File:Tatsuta-taisha, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |location = {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|WD=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}{{ill|Sangō, Nara|en|Sangō, Nara|WD=Q1203139|lt=Sangō, Nara|1=Sangō, Nara}} Tateno Minami 1-chome 29-1 |ISO = JP-29 |lat_deg = 34|lat_min = 35|lat_sec = 35.1 |lon_deg = 135|lon_min = 41|lon_sec = 14.4 |deity = {{ill|Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}
{{ill|Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|国御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 2 seats)
{{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|en|Twenty-Two Shrines|WD=Q215320|lt=Twenty-Two Shrines|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} (Middle Seven Shrines)
Former {{ill|Kanpei Taisha|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei Taisha|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}}
{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} |established = Traditionally during the reign of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} |main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |annual_festival = {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4|1=April 4}} |rituals = Taki Festival ({{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}})
Fūchin Grand Festival (1st Sunday of July)
Autumn Grand Festival (3rd Saturday and Sunday of October) |map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-taisha, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|{{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}}}}]] '''Tatsuta Taisha''' (龍田大社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tateno Minami, {{ill|Sangō, Nara|en|Sangō, Nara|WD=Q1203139|lt=Sangō, Nara|1=Sangō, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|WD=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and one of the {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|en|Twenty-Two Shrines|WD=Q215320|lt=Twenty-Two Shrines|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} (Middle Seven Shrines). Under the former shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a {{ill|Kanpei Taisha|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei Taisha|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}}, and it is currently listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It was formerly known as "Tatsuta Shrine." The shrine has long been venerated as the home of the deity of {{ill|Wind|en|Wind|WD=Q8094|lt=Wind|1=Wind}} ({{ill|Fūjin|en|Fūjin|WD=Q1483957|lt=Fūjin|1=Fūjin}}).[http://yamatoji.nara-kankou.or.jp/01shaji/01jinja/02west_area/tatsutataisha/] == History == == Overview == According to the "{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|Norito|en|Norito|WD=Q1760119|lt=Norito|1=Norito}} in the "Tatsuta Wind God Festival Norito," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, years of poor harvests and widespread epidemics prompted the emperor to personally pray to the heavenly and earthly deities. In a dream, he received a divine message instructing him to enshrine the two deities, {{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja|国御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, on Mount Tatsuta, which led to the founding of the shrine. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, the earliest record appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}," stating that on April 10, the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (675), an imperial envoy was dispatched to enshrine the Wind God at Tatsuta-Tateno and the Great Taboo God at Hirose Kawamuki. The "{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyocho|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|Encho era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encho era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} (927), lists "Tatsuta-no-Iwato-no-Ame-no-Mihashira-Kuni-no-Mihashira Jinja" with two deities in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}'s {{ill|Heguri District|ja|平群郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri District|1=Heguri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483501}}. They are classified as {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and received offerings from the imperial court during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Kanpei Taisha|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei Taisha|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} in 1871 (4th year of Meiji) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In 1948 (23rd year of Showa), it was included as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In June 2020 (2nd year of Reiwa), Tatsuta Taisha, along with the {{ill|Tatsuta Ancient Road|ja|龍田古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Ancient Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kame-no-se|ja|亀の瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kame-no-se|1=Q11370702|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370702}}, was designated as a {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|WD=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage Site|1=Japan Heritage}}. [https://kamenose.jp/] The shrine is also famous as a scenic spot for {{ill|Autumn leaf color|en|Autumn leaf color|WD=Q1156320|lt=autumn foliage|1=Autumn leaf color}}. The deity enshrined in the auxiliary shrine, Tatsuta Hime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|Tatsuta-hime|ja|龍田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竜田姫|1=Q3513454|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3513454}}), is revered as a goddess of autumn and has been celebrated in many poems since ancient times. === Ranks of Deities === * 13th year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin era|1=Kōnin (era)}} (822), August 3: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (from "{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}") - Referred to as "Tatsuta no Kami." * 3rd year of {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō era|1=Kashō}} (850), July 11: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (from "{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}") - Referred to as "Tatsuta Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto" and "Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto." * 2nd year of {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju era|1=Ninju}} (852), July 25: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (from "Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku") - Referred to as "Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto" and "Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto." * 2nd year of Ninju (852), October 2: Junior Third Rank (from "Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku") - Referred to as "Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto" and "Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto." * 3rd year of {{ill|Ten'an era|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an era|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} (859), January 27: Promoted from Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank (from "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") - Referred to as "Tatsuta no Kami." == See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|en|Twenty-Two Shrines|WD=Q215320|lt=Twenty-Two Shrines|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * {{ill|Tatsuta Maru|en|Tatsuta Maru|WD=Q2553643|lt=Tatsuta Maru|1=Tatsuta Maru}} ==Notes== {{reflist|2}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]]{{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|龍田大社|version=102008727|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:龍田大社]] [[en:Tatsuta Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-taisha}} [[de:Tatsuta-Taisha]] [[fr:Tatsuta-taisha]] [[ru:Тацута-тайся]] [[sv:Tatsuta-taisha]] [[zh:龍田大社]] {{Commons category|Tatsuta-taisha}} [[de:Tatsuta-Taisha]] [[fr:Tatsuta-taisha]] [[ru:Тацута-тайся]] [[sv:Tatsuta-taisha]] [[zh:龍田大社]] {{wikidata link|Q246455}} [[Category:Architecture of Misato Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-zukuri]] [[Category:Municipal History of Misato]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tatsuta-taisha]][[de:Tatsuta-Taisha]][[en:Tatsuta Taisha]][[fr:Tatsuta-taisha]][[ja:龍田大社]][[ru:Тацута-тайся]][[sv:Tatsuta-taisha]][[zh:龍田大社]] [[co:commons:Category:Tatsuta-taisha]][[de:Tatsuta-Taisha]][[en:Tatsuta Taisha]][[fr:Tatsuta-taisha]][[ja:龍田大社]][[ru:Тацута-тайся]][[sv:Tatsuta-taisha]][[zh:龍田大社]] [[de:Tatsuta-Taisha]][[en:Tatsuta Taisha]][[fr:Tatsuta-taisha]][[ja:龍田大社]][[ru:Тацута-тайся]][[sv:Tatsuta-taisha]][[zh:龍田大社]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Tatsuta Shrine'''|龍田大社|Tatsuta-taisha}} | image = File:Tatsuta-Taisha02.JPG | caption = [[Torii]] at the entrance of Tatsuta Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.59305556|N|135.6875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767|1=Shinto}} | type = | deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|WD=Q1073925|1=Shinatsuhiko}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Sango|WD=Q1203139|1=Sangō, Nara}} | part_of= {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|WD=Q215320|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tatsuta Shrine'''|龍田大社|Tatsuta-taisha}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sangō, Nara|Sangō]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara|1=Nara Prefecture}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine is also known in Japanese (esp. formerly) as {{nihongo3||龍田神社|Tatsuta-jinja}}. The Shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.] In 965, {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}} ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''kami'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Tatsuta Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116-117. From 1871 through 1946, the Tatsuta Shrine was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. ==Overview== == Tatsuta Taisha == {{otheruses|Shrine in Sangō, Ikoma District, Nara Prefecture|Shrine in Ikaruga, Ikoma District, Nara Prefecture|{{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|lt=Tatsuta Shrine|ja|龍田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|qq=}}}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tatsuta Taisha |image = [[File:Tatsuta-taisha, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden

{{ill|Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja|国御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni no Mihashira no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} 2 seats)

{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} |established = Traditionally during the reign of the 10th {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} |main_hall = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |annual_festival = {{ill|April 4|en|April 4|WD=Q2508|lt=April 4|1=April 4}} |rituals = Taki Festival ({{ill|April 3|en|April 3|WD=Q2509|lt=April 3|1=April 3}})
Fūchin Grand Festival (1st Sunday of July)
Autumn Grand Festival (3rd Saturday and Sunday of October) |map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-taisha, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|{{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}}}}]] '''Tatsuta Taisha''' (龍田大社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tateno Minami, {{ill|Sangō, Nara|en|Sangō, Nara|WD=Q1203139|lt=Sangō, Nara|1=Sangō, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|WD=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and one of the {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|en|Twenty-Two Shrines|WD=Q215320|lt=Twenty-Two Shrines|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} (Middle Seven Shrines). Under the former shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a {{ill|Kanpei Taisha|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei Taisha|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}}, and it is currently listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It was formerly known as "Tatsuta Shrine." The shrine has long been venerated as the home of the deity of {{ill|Wind|en|Wind|WD=Q8094|lt=Wind|1=Wind}} ({{ill|Fūjin|en|Fūjin|WD=Q1483957|lt=Fūjin|1=Fūjin}}).[http://yamatoji.nara-kankou.or.jp/01shaji/01jinja/02west_area/tatsutataisha/] == History == == Overview == According to the "{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|Norito|en|Norito|WD=Q1760119|lt=Norito|1=Norito}} in the "Tatsuta Wind God Festival Norito," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, years of poor harvests and widespread epidemics prompted the emperor to personally pray to the heavenly and earthly deities. In a dream, he received a divine message instructing him to enshrine the two deities, {{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja|国御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, on Mount Tatsuta, which led to the founding of the shrine. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, the earliest record appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}," stating that on April 10, the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (675), an imperial envoy was dispatched to enshrine the Wind God at Tatsuta-Tateno and the Great Taboo God at Hirose Kawamuki. The "{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyocho|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|Encho era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encho era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} (927), lists "Tatsuta-no-Iwato-no-Ame-no-Mihashira-Kuni-no-Mihashira Jinja" with two deities in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}'s {{ill|Heguri District|ja|平群郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri District|1=Heguri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483501}}. They are classified as {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and received offerings from the imperial court during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Kanpei Taisha|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei Taisha|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} in 1871 (4th year of Meiji) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In 1948 (23rd year of Showa), it was included as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In June 2020 (2nd year of Reiwa), Tatsuta Taisha, along with the {{ill|Tatsuta Ancient Road|ja|龍田古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Ancient Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kame-no-se|ja|亀の瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kame-no-se|1=Q11370702|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370702}}, was designated as a {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|WD=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage Site|1=Japan Heritage}}. [https://kamenose.jp/] The shrine is also famous as a scenic spot for {{ill|Autumn leaf color|en|Autumn leaf color|WD=Q1156320|lt=autumn foliage|1=Autumn leaf color}}. The deity enshrined in the auxiliary shrine, Tatsuta Hime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|Tatsuta-hime|ja|龍田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竜田姫|1=Q3513454|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3513454}}), is revered as a goddess of autumn and has been celebrated in many poems since ancient times. === Ranks of Deities === * 13th year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin era|1=Kōnin (era)}} (822), August 3: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (from "{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}") - Referred to as "Tatsuta no Kami." * 3rd year of {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō era|1=Kashō}} (850), July 11: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (from "{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}") - Referred to as "Tatsuta Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto" and "Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto." * 2nd year of {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju era|1=Ninju}} (852), July 25: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (from "Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku") - Referred to as "Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto" and "Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto." * 2nd year of Ninju (852), October 2: Junior Third Rank (from "Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku") - Referred to as "Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto" and "Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto." * 3rd year of {{ill|Ten'an era|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an era|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} (859), January 27: Promoted from Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank (from "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") - Referred to as "Tatsuta no Kami." == See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} * {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|en|Twenty-Two Shrines|WD=Q215320|lt=Twenty-Two Shrines|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} * [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]] * {{ill|Tatsuta Maru|en|Tatsuta Maru|WD=Q2553643|lt=Tatsuta Maru|1=Tatsuta Maru}} ==Notes== {{reflist|2}} ==References== * [[John Breen (scholar)|Breen, John]] and [[Mark Teeuwen]]. (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=nijunisha ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami.''] Honolulu: [[:en:University of Hawaii Press|University of Hawaii Press]]. {{ISBN|978-0-8248-2363-4}} * {{ill|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|en|Richard Ponsonby-Fane|WD=Q3246771|lt=Ponsonby-Fane, Richard|1=Richard Ponsonby-Fane}}. (1962). [https://books.google.com/books?id=tjEvAAAAYAAJ&q=Studies+in+Shinto+and+Shrines ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/3994492 OCLC 399449] * ____________. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] {{Beppyo Shrines}}]]{{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|龍田大社|version=102008727|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tatsuta-taisha}} {{Commons category|Tatsuta-taisha}} {{wikidata link|Q246455|ja|龍田大社|en|Tatsuta Taisha|de|Tatsuta-Taisha|fr|Tatsuta-taisha|ru|Тацута-тайся|sv|Tatsuta-taisha|zh|龍田大社}} [[Category:Architecture of Misato Town]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nara prefecture]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Kasuga-zukuri]] [[Category:Municipal History of Misato]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Taisha]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Twenty-Two Shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Junior Third Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Nara Prefecture, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = {{nihongo|'''Tatsuta Shrine'''|龍田大社|Tatsuta-taisha}} | image = File:Tatsuta-Taisha02.JPG | caption = [[Torii]] at the entrance of Tatsuta Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.59305556|N|135.6875|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|WD=Q812767|1=Shinto}} | type = | deity = {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|WD=Q1073925|1=Shinatsuhiko}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = | prefecture= {{ill|Sango|WD=Q1203139|1=Sangō, Nara}} | part_of= {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|WD=Q215320|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} }} {{nihongo|'''Tatsuta Shrine'''|龍田大社|Tatsuta-taisha}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[Sangō, Nara|Sangō]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara|1=Nara Prefecture}} in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine is also known in Japanese (esp. formerly) as {{nihongo3||龍田神社|Tatsuta-jinja}}. The Shrine became the object of Imperial patronage during the early {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}.Breen, John ''et al.'' (2000). [https://books.google.com/books?id=MADlfH002mAC&q=oharano ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami,'' pp. 74-75.] In 965, {{ill|Emperor Murakami|en|Emperor Murakami|WD=Q374015|lt=Emperor Murakami|1=Emperor Murakami}} ordered that Imperial messengers were sent to report important events to the guardian ''kami'' of Japan. These ''heihaku'' were initially presented to 16 shrines including the Tatsuta Shrine.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1962). ''Studies in Shinto and Shrines,'' pp. 116-117. From 1871 through 1946, the Tatsuta Shrine was officially designated one of the {{nihongo|''[[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines|Kanpei-taisha]]''|官幣大社}}, meaning that it stood in the first Rank of government supported shrines.Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 124. ==Overview== == Tatsuta Taisha == {{otheruses|Shrine in Sangō, Ikoma District, Nara Prefecture|Shrine in Ikaruga, Ikoma District, Nara Prefecture|{{ill|Tatsuta Taisha|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|lt=Tatsuta Shrine|ja|龍田神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Shrine|qq=}}}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Tatsuta Taisha |image = [[File:Tatsuta-taisha, haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden

Fūchin Grand Festival (1st Sunday of July)
Autumn Grand Festival (3rd Saturday and Sunday of October) |map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tatsuta-taisha, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|{{center|{{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}}}}]] '''Tatsuta Taisha''' (龍田大社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tateno Minami, {{ill|Sangō, Nara|en|Sangō, Nara|WD=Q1203139|lt=Sangō, Nara|1=Sangō, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|WD=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and one of the {{ill|Twenty-Two Shrines|en|Twenty-Two Shrines|WD=Q215320|lt=Twenty-Two Shrines|1=Twenty-Two Shrines}} (Middle Seven Shrines). Under the former shrine Ranking system, it was designated as a {{ill|Kanpei Taisha|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei Taisha|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}}, and it is currently listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It was formerly known as "Tatsuta Shrine." The shrine has long been venerated as the home of the deity of {{ill|Wind|en|Wind|WD=Q8094|lt=Wind|1=Wind}} ({{ill|Fūjin|en|Fūjin|WD=Q1483957|lt=Fūjin|1=Fūjin}}).[http://yamatoji.nara-kankou.or.jp/01shaji/01jinja/02west_area/tatsutataisha/] == History == == Overview == According to the "{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|Norito|en|Norito|WD=Q1760119|lt=Norito|1=Norito}} in the "Tatsuta Wind God Festival Norito," during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}, years of poor harvests and widespread epidemics prompted the emperor to personally pray to the heavenly and earthly deities. In a dream, he received a divine message instructing him to enshrine the two deities, {{ill|Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja|天御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}} and {{ill|Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja|国御柱命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シナツヒコ|1=Shinatsuhiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1073925}}, on Mount Tatsuta, which led to the founding of the shrine. In the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|WD=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, the earliest record appears in the "{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}," stating that on April 10, the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} (675), an imperial envoy was dispatched to enshrine the Wind God at Tatsuta-Tateno and the Great Taboo God at Hirose Kawamuki. The "{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinmyocho|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, compiled in the 5th year of {{ill|Encho era|ja|延長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Encho era|1=Enchō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q97379316}} (927), lists "Tatsuta-no-Iwato-no-Ame-no-Mihashira-Kuni-no-Mihashira Jinja" with two deities in {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|WD=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}'s {{ill|Heguri District|ja|平群郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri District|1=Heguri district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11483501}}. They are classified as {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} and received offerings from the imperial court during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Kanpei Taisha|ja|官幣大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanpei Taisha|1=Kanpei Taisha (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10209769}} in 1871 (4th year of Meiji) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In 1948 (23rd year of Showa), it was included as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} under the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. In June 2020 (2nd year of Reiwa), Tatsuta Taisha, along with the {{ill|Tatsuta Ancient Road|ja|龍田古道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta Ancient Road|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Kame-no-se|ja|亀の瀬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kame-no-se|1=Q11370702|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370702}}, was designated as a {{ill|Japan Heritage|en|Japan Heritage|WD=Q18382798|lt=Japan Heritage Site|1=Japan Heritage}}. [https://kamenose.jp/] The shrine is also famous as a scenic spot for {{ill|Autumn leaf color|en|Autumn leaf color|WD=Q1156320|lt=autumn foliage|1=Autumn leaf color}}. The deity enshrined in the auxiliary shrine, Tatsuta Hime-no-Mikoto ({{ill|Tatsuta-hime|ja|龍田姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuta-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 竜田姫|1=Q3513454|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3513454}}), is revered as a goddess of autumn and has been celebrated in many poems since ancient times. === Ranks of Deities === * 13th year of {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|WD=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin era|1=Kōnin (era)}} (822), August 3: Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade (from "{{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|WD=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}") - Referred to as "Tatsuta no Kami." * 3rd year of {{ill|Kashō|en|Kashō|WD=Q1373157|lt=Kashō era|1=Kashō}} (850), July 11: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade (from "{{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}") - Referred to as "Tatsuta Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto" and "Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto." * 2nd year of {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju era|1=Ninju}} (852), July 25: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade (from "Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku") - Referred to as "Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto" and "Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto." * 2nd year of Ninju (852), October 2: Junior Third Rank (from "Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku") - Referred to as "Ame-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto" and "Kuni-no-Mihashira-no-Mikoto." * 3rd year of {{ill|Ten'an era|ja|天安|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ten'an era|1=Cheonan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3577288}} (859), January 27: Promoted from Junior Third Rank to Senior Third Rank (from "{{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}") -
